stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Olga Tokarczuk was awarded the 2018 Nobel Prize in Literature.
  • Tokarczuk was born in Sulechów in western Poland and currently lives in Wrocław.
  • Many readers have singled out Tokarczuk’s 2007 book Flights for praise.
  • Flights is a fragmentary novel about travel, anatomy, and the human body.
stem: The student wants to introduce Flights to an audience unfamiliar with the novel and its author. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Olga Tokarczuk, who wrote Flights and later was awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature, was born in Sulechów in western Poland and currently lives in Wrocław.
A much-praised fragmentary novel about travel, anatomy, and the human body, Flights (2007) was written by Olga Tokarczuk, winner of the 2018 Nobel Prize in Literature.
Many readers have singled out Olga Tokarczuk’s 2007 book Flights, a fragmentary novel about travel, anatomy, and the human body, for praise.
Flights is a fragmentary novel about travel, anatomy, and the human body by Olga Tokarczuk, who was born in western Poland.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. It introduces Flights with its subject and identifies its author as the 2018 Nobel laureate, giving an unfamiliar audience the background needed for both.

Choice A introduces Tokarczuk but doesn’t effectively introduce Flights. Choices C and D provide background but don’t identify the author’s Nobel status for an audience unfamiliar with her.
